steps.title: Isolate the variable term
steps.content:
Subtract $7c$ from both sides of the equation:
$$b^2+7c=4d$$
$$b^2=4d-7c$$

steps.title: Check the radical value
steps.content:
The condition $$d>\frac{7}{4}c$$ makes the expression under the radical positive:
$$4d>7c$$
$$4d-7c>0$$

steps.title: Include both signs
steps.content:
A positive number has both a positive and a negative square root, so:
$$b=\pm\sqrt{4d-7c}$$
The answer is C.
